description: A designed patriotic propaganda/slogan poster in vertical (portrait) format. The photographic background shows the cavernous interior of a large industrial warehouse or factory floor with exposed steel roof trusses, rows of overhead lights, support columns, and scattered equipment, rendered in desaturated near-black-and-white tones. The single splash of full color is a large American flag hanging vertically from the ceiling truss in the center of the frame, drawing the eye. Centered across the lower-middle in an elegant white serif (engraved/classical capitals) font is the slogan 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST'. The selective-color technique (gray industrial setting plus a vivid red-white-and-blue flag) is a common nationalist design motif used to evoke patriotism and the dignity of American industry/manufacturing. No agency seal is visible on the image; it is attributed via the USDOL handle. Target audience: the general public and the administration's base, reinforcing the 'America First' labor agenda of reshoring manufacturing and prioritizing American workers.
